title: MVP implementation orchestration
close_reason: All child MVP beads myrivo-1.1 through myrivo-1.6 were completed and merged.

title: Bootstrap monorepo foundation (Next.js, Turborepo, quality gates)
notes: Completed monorepo bootstrap, CI gates, workspace scripts, and initial web scaffold with passing lint/typecheck/test/build.

title: Implement Supabase schema, migrations, and tenant RLS
notes: Completed initial Supabase schema migration, RLS policies, and seed scaffolding.

title: Implement auth and merchant store bootstrap flow
notes:
Started store bootstrap API and tenant utility scaffolding; auth UX and session-bound flows pending.
Implemented Tailwind tokenized UI foundation, single-component-per-file page composition, centralized pricing config, and automated Vercel deployment workflow.
Completed Supabase project initialization and linking, pushed initial migration, and implemented server-authenticated merchant onboarding (signup/login/callback/store bootstrap).

title: Implement product catalog and inventory management MVP
notes: Starting product catalog and inventory UI/API hardening on top of authenticated tenant context.
close_reason: Completed tenant-safe product catalog and inventory management MVP (dashboard UI + secured API create/update/list).

title: Implement Stripe subscription billing and checkout webhooks
close_reason: Completed secure Stripe checkout session creation and subscription webhook lifecycle synchronization.

title: Production deployment wiring (Vercel) and operations docs
notes:
Added explicit Vercel scope and project-access preflight in production deploy workflow to fix project resolution in CI.
Reworked deploy workflow to runtime-link Vercel project, eliminating static org/project ID dependency in CI.
Added vercel.json framework override (nextjs) to fix Vercel output directory detection during CI deploy.
Adjusted Vercel deploy workflow to run from apps/web via --cwd for monorepo output correctness.
Added apps/web/vercel.json framework override to ensure Vercel CLI treats workspace as Next.js.
Set turbopack.root to monorepo root (../..) for stable Next.js resolution in CI.
Made turbopack.root conditional: apps/web on Vercel, monorepo root locally.
Set VERCEL=1 for vercel build step so conditional turbopack root matches CI behavior.
Switched web build script to webpack mode for stable CI/Vercel builds.
Set vercel build to --prod to match prebuilt deploy target environment.
Switched production deploy to direct vercel deploy --prod (no prebuilt artifacts).
close_reason: Completed Vercel production release workflow hardening, including main-only controlled deploys and stable build pathing.

title: Expand tests for billing, avatar, domains, and dashboard critical flows
description: Add/upgrade targeted tests for newly shipped billing/domain/avatar/dashboard flows to prevent regressions and improve confidence for future refactors.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Second hardening wave: API robustness and architecture cleanup
description: Run a second bounded pass after the first hardening wave to remove remaining parse/error and hook-safety debt and improve component architecture where still oversized.

title: Roll out safe JSON body parsing across remaining mutation routes
description: Apply shared safe JSON parsing helper to remaining API mutation routes that still call request.json directly, returning deterministic 400 responses for malformed payloads.

title: Remove remaining hook dependency suppression in dashboard email subscribers
description: Replace hook-suppression with transition-safe logic in store email subscribers manager and verify behavior.

title: Modularize product manager domain types/utilities into dedicated module
description: Extract product manager domain types and pure helpers into a dedicated module for maintainability and future testing expansion without changing behavior.

title: Customer account management UX hardening (saved stores/items actions + optimistic updates)
description: Add robust management actions on /account for saved stores and saved items, with optimistic state updates, loading/error handling, and focused test coverage.
close_reason: Implemented customer account saved-store/item management UX with API hardening and tests

title: Customer account active cart management (clear from dashboard)
description: Allow signed-in customers to clear active carts directly from account dashboard, with API support, optimistic UX, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented active cart removal API + account dashboard remove action with tests

title: UX consistency hardening wave (dashboard IA/layout/actions/states)
description: Standardize dashboard page structure, navigation behavior, card hierarchy, action bars, and state feedback patterns for consistent and intuitive UX.
close_reason: Completed scaffold/action-bar standardization, core page migration, and nav normalization

title: Dashboard scaffold primitives and action-bar standardization
description: Add shared dashboard page scaffold primitive and standardize action bar behavior within scroll container to eliminate overlap/inset inconsistencies.
close_reason: Implemented dashboard scaffold primitive and standardized in-container sticky form action bar

title: Core page migration to scaffold (overview/catalog/orders/billing)
description: Migrate highest-traffic dashboard pages to the new scaffold and unify header/body/action hierarchy.
close_reason: Migrated overview/catalog/orders/billing to dashboard page scaffold and normalized core page hierarchy

title: Navigation model normalization (persistent primary + contextual secondary)
description: Refactor dashboard nav to keep primary navigation visible while exposing contextual child links for active workspace.
close_reason: Normalized dashboard nav to persistent primary links with contextual secondary workspace links

title: Notifications Program: Full Buildout
description: Build the complete app notification system across in-app inbox, email delivery orchestration, preference enforcement, event triggers, digest delivery, observability, and rollout controls for owner/customer experiences.
notes:
Execution order:
1) myrivo-20 schema+types
2) myrivo-21 catalog/dispatcher/preferences core
3) myrivo-22 owner triggers + myrivo-19 inventory/system event emitters
4) myrivo-23 inbox APIs
5) myrivo-24 dashboard notification UX
6) myrivo-26 settings/preferences expansion
7) myrivo-25 reliability/dedupe/throttling
8) myrivo-27 weekly digest
9) myrivo-28 observability/admin reporting

Definition of done:
- In-app owner inbox with unread counts and read/dismiss flows
- Preference-aware channel routing
- Existing transactional emails preserved for required lifecycle messages
- Dedupe/retry behavior covered by tests
- Notification delivery metrics and failure visibility available to operators

title: Inventory + Setup/System Alert Events
description: Generate notification events for inventory thresholds and store setup/system conditions (domain, checkout, payments readiness, shipping exceptions) and route via dispatcher. Avoid alert fatigue with throttling/dedupe windows.
notes: Implemented inventory + setup warning notification events with dedupe-window controls. Added notifications config module (NOTIFICATIONS_LOW_STOCK_THRESHOLD, NOTIFICATIONS_ALERT_DEDUPE_WINDOW_HOURS) and dedupe-bucket helper. Extended owner-notifications with notifyOwnersInventoryLevel (threshold crossing + out-of-stock detection, recipient fanout, dedupe keying) and notifyOwnersSystemSetupWarning (missing setup steps, deduped warnings, actor exclusion). Wired inventory alerts into product PATCH flow by comparing previous vs updated inventory and status. Wired setup warning dispatch into onboarding launch endpoint when launch readiness fails. Added unit tests in owner-notifications.test.ts for low-stock transition, no-spam low->low, out-of-stock transition, and setup warning dispatch. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- owner-notifications.test.ts user-profile-route.test.ts notifications-route.test.ts notifications-actions-route.test.ts store-members-accept-route.test.ts.

title: Full multi-tenant runtime cutover
description: Replace single-store runtime assumptions with membership-driven tenant context across dashboard, APIs, and storefront flows. Preserve safety guards for destructive scripts.
acceptance_criteria: All authenticated flows resolve active store via membership context, dashboard supports switching stores, and single-store env is no longer required for runtime tenant selection.

title: Tenant context resolver and membership model hardening
description: Add canonical active-store resolver for server runtime using membership + selected store cookie, remove owner-store slug pinning.
acceptance_criteria: Server helper resolves one active store for authenticated user from membership and selected slug; explicit errors for no memberships and invalid selected store.

title: Dashboard multi-store switcher
description: Add UI + API to select active store and persist via httpOnly cookie.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard header shows switcher and store status; selecting store updates cookie and all dashboard pages read selected store.

title: API route tenant enforcement migration
description: Move API handlers to membership-based store resolution and remove single-store env dependence.
acceptance_criteria: All authenticated API handlers scope to resolved active store; cross-tenant access blocked.

title: Storefront slug and checkout/cart tenant resolution
description: Ensure storefront APIs/pages resolve store by slug/path and no longer rely on single env slug.
acceptance_criteria: Storefront, cart, checkout, newsletter and promotion preview all use explicit store slug context.

title: Test and migration hardening for multi-tenant
description: Add regression tests for multi-store selection/isolation; add migration to relax owner-only membership constraint.
acceptance_criteria: Unit/integration/e2e cover multi-store behavior; DB migration allows membership roles and keeps owner compatibility.

title: Docs and operational runbooks for multi-tenant
description: Update architecture and env docs for multi-tenant runtime and test-store safety.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain tenant selection, test store safety, and local workflow for switching stores.

title: Notifications Data Model + Migrations
description: Create notification persistence schema: notifications table, notification_delivery_attempts, indices, retention strategy hooks, and typed database contracts. Include migration/backfill plan and idempotency/dedupe key support.
notes: Implemented notifications foundation schema and typed contracts. Added tables public.notifications and public.notification_delivery_attempts with status/severity/channel checks, dedupe unique index on (recipient_user_id, dedupe_key), delivery attempt indices, updated_at trigger, and recipient-scoped RLS policies. Added NotificationRecord and NotificationDeliveryAttemptRecord types in apps/web/types/database.ts. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), supabase db push (applied 20260307030000), supabase migration list confirms local=remote.

title: Notification Catalog, Dispatcher, and Preference Resolver
description: Implement notification domain core in lib/notifications: event catalog, severity defaults, channel target defaults, preference resolver, dedupe logic, and dispatcher orchestration for in-app + email channels.
notes: Implemented notification core modules: lib/notifications/catalog.ts, preferences.ts, dispatcher.ts. Added event catalog defaults by category, expanded account preference resolver with backward-compatible defaults, channel resolution logic, dedupe-aware dispatch insertion, and optional email delivery attempt recording. Added tests: notification-preferences.test.ts and notification-dispatcher-channels.test.ts. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), npm test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- notification-preferences.test.ts notification-dispatcher-channels.test.ts (pass).

title: Owner Event Triggers: Orders, Shipping, Pickup, Team
description: Wire owner notification trigger adapters into existing workflows: order created, order shipping updates, pickup state changes, and team/invite acceptance actions. Emit structured notification events through dispatcher with stable dedupe keys.
notes: Implemented owner notification triggers using dispatcher via new adapter lib/notifications/owner-notifications.ts. Wired into order flow (sendOrderCreatedNotifications + sendOrderShippingNotification) and team invite acceptance route. Added in-app owner events with stable dedupe keys for order.created.owner, order.pickup.updated, order.fulfillment.shipped, order.fulfillment.delivered, and team.invite.accepted. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web (pass), npm test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- store-members-accept-route.test.ts order-email-pickup-summary.test.ts notification-preferences.test.ts notification-dispatcher-channels.test.ts (pass).

title: Notifications Inbox API + Read/Dismiss Endpoints
description: Add server APIs for listing notifications, unread counts, mark-read, mark-all-read, and dismiss actions with store/user authorization checks and pagination.

title: Dashboard Notifications UI (Bell, Feed, Inbox Page)
description: Build owner in-app notification UX: header bell with unread badge, notifications feed panel/page, read-state interactions, severity treatments, and action links routed to relevant dashboard surfaces.

title: Delivery Reliability, Dedupe, and Throttling
description: Implement notification dedupe keys, retry behavior, backoff policy, and throttling windows to prevent duplicate or spammy alerts. Ensure idempotent dispatch when routes/webhooks are retried.
notes: Implemented dispatcher reliability controls. Added automatic idempotency dedupe key generation when dedupeKey is not provided (bucketed by configurable idempotency window and stable payload hash). Added per-recipient/event throttling window checks before insert (configurable max-per-window and window duration). Added email delivery retry with exponential backoff and configurable attempt/delay values. Added per-attempt notification_delivery_attempts rows with attempt metadata. Added missing-email failure handling for email-targeted dispatches and final status update logic after retries. Added tests in notification-dispatcher-reliability.test.ts for throttle skipping and retry-to-success behavior. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- notification-dispatcher-reliability.test.ts notification-dispatcher-channels.test.ts.

title: Notification Preferences Expansion + Settings UX
description: Expand user account notification preference schema beyond weekly digest/product announcements to category/channel controls. Update API validation, defaults, migration behavior, and settings UI for owner notification controls.

title: Weekly Digest Pipeline
description: Implement scheduled weekly digest generation and email dispatch for eligible users/stores. Respect preferences, include key KPI summaries + actionable links, and log delivery attempts.
notes: Implemented weekly digest pipeline. Added lib/notifications/digest/weekly.ts service that computes prior-week store KPIs (paid orders, paid revenue, pending fulfillment, low-stock counts), resolves eligible recipients (active owner/admin/staff with weeklyDigestEmails enabled), and dispatches digest.weekly notifications with dedupe key per store+recipient+week. Added cron-oriented endpoint POST /api/notifications/digest/weekly with optional bearer secret (NOTIFICATIONS_CRON_SECRET). Added digest content builder helper and weekly-digest.test.ts coverage for KPI email body generation and report link output. Validation: npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- weekly-digest.test.ts.

title: Notification Observability + Admin Reporting
description: Add metrics/logging for notification volume, failures, and latency by channel/event type. Provide admin/reporting surfaces for troubleshooting and delivery health.

title: Reviews Platform: Store + Product Reviews with Multi-Image Support
description:
Objective
Build a production-grade reviews system for both stores and products, including multiple review images, moderation, trust controls, owner responses, SEO-ready rendering, and operational observability.

Scope
- Verified purchase-aware review submission for store and product contexts
- Multi-image upload and attachment per review
- Moderation pipeline for text and media
- Storefront display with filtering/sorting/pagination
- Dashboard moderation and response workflow
- Notification integration and digest hooks
- SEO structured data integration
- Anti-abuse controls and audit trails

Out of Scope (initial release)
- Third-party review syndication imports
- AI-generated auto-replies
- Public reviewer profiles

Success Criteria
- Owners can moderate and respond to reviews from dashboard
- Customers can submit verified reviews with multiple images
- Storefront displays performant, paginated, filterable reviews
- Review aggregates are exposed safely for SEO
- Abuse and spam controls prevent low-effort attack vectors
acceptance_criteria:
- End-to-end review flow works for store and product review types
- Multiple images per review supported with deterministic ordering
- Moderation statuses enforced in read APIs and UI
- Structured data for aggregate ratings validates in testing
- Lint, typecheck, tests pass for changed surfaces

title: Define reviews domain spec, API contracts, and moderation state machine
description:
Create canonical design doc for reviews architecture, data model, moderation lifecycle, trust signals, and API shape.

Deliverables
- Review entity model (store/product variants, ownership, verification)
- Moderation state machine and transitions
- API contract definitions (submit/read/moderate/respond/media upload)
- Permission matrix (customer/owner/admin/support)
- Data retention and deletion behavior
- Performance targets for list queries and aggregates
acceptance_criteria:
- Design doc reviewed and linked in issue notes
- State machine includes explicit invalid transitions
- All API payloads include validation rules and error semantics
notes: Created canonical spec: docs/reviews-platform-architecture-spec.md (domain model, moderation state machine, API contracts, permission matrix, validation limits, anti-abuse, SEO, acceptance mapping).

title: SEO integration: aggregate rating and review schema output
description:
Expose safe structured data for product/store review aggregates.

Requirements
- AggregateRating + Review schema where eligible
- Only approved/published reviews included
- Guardrails for minimum-review thresholds if configured
- Validation docs for rich result testing
acceptance_criteria:
- Structured data validates in Google rich result test fixtures
- No unpublished/rejected reviews leak into schema payloads

title: Anti-abuse controls, rate limits, and moderation heuristics
description:
Add abuse prevention for review submission and media upload.

Controls
- Per-IP and per-identity submission rate limits
- Duplicate-text heuristics and suspicious-pattern scoring
- Content sanitization and profanity/blocked-term checks
- Optional hold-for-review flags
- Audit metadata for abuse decisions
acceptance_criteria:
- Rate limits enforced with deterministic error codes
- Suspicious submissions route to moderation queue
- Abuse-related actions are auditable
notes: Implemented anti-abuse foundation: apps/web/lib/reviews/abuse.ts (fingerprinting, blocked term heuristics, suspicious-pattern detection, configurable limits), env wiring in apps/web/lib/env.ts and .env.example, and tests in apps/web/tests/review-abuse.test.ts. Remaining for bead completion: wire enforcement into review submission APIs and persist abuse/audit decisions.

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Wired anti-abuse into submission pipeline via POST /api/reviews: per-IP and per-email rate limits with deterministic error codes, duplicate fingerprint guard, content sanitization, moderation heuristics hold, and persisted abuse audit metadata (fingerprint, reasons, ip hash, user-agent) on reviews.metadata. Added helper utilities/tests for IP parsing, signal hashing, and rate-limit enforcement in apps/web/lib/reviews/abuse.ts and apps/web/tests/review-abuse.test.ts.

title: Comprehensive test matrix for reviews backend and UI
description:
Add robust test coverage for schema, APIs, permissions, rendering, and moderation behavior.

Coverage
- Unit tests for validation and state transitions
- API integration tests for submit/read/moderate/respond
- Permission/RLS tests
- UI tests for storefront and dashboard interactions
- Regression tests for legacy route/link compatibility
acceptance_criteria:
- High-confidence coverage on critical review workflows
- No regressions in notification routing or route resolution

title: Observability and operational health for reviews
description:
Add metrics, error tracking context, and admin health visibility.

Includes
- Counters for submissions, approvals, rejections, low-star volume
- Upload failure/error telemetry
- Queue depth and latency indicators
- Runbook notes for incident triage
acceptance_criteria:
- Review pipeline health visible from platform ops surface
- On-call runbook updated with review incident paths

title: Rollout plan, feature gating strategy, and backfill scripts
description:
Prepare release path for staged launch of reviews feature.

Deliverables
- Environment gating and phased enablement plan
- Data backfill scripts for aggregate snapshots
- Migration/rollback strategy and smoke checklist
- Post-launch verification checklist
acceptance_criteria:
- Rollout checklist documented and testable in staging
- Backfill and rollback procedures validated

title: Create database schema for reviews, media, responses, and aggregates
description:
Implement migrations for core review storage and queryable aggregates.

Schema
- reviews table (store/product/order linkage, rating/title/body, verification, status, timestamps)
- review_media table (review_id, storage path/url, sort order, dimensions, metadata)
- review_responses table (owner response text, timestamps)
- Optional aggregate snapshots/materialized view for rating rollups
- Indexes for storefront reads and moderation queues
- RLS policies for customer create/read and owner moderation workflows
acceptance_criteria:
- Migrations apply cleanly up/down in local environment
- Index strategy supports paginated storefront reads
- RLS blocks unauthorized moderation or unpublished reads
notes: Implemented migration: supabase/migrations/20260307143000_reviews_foundation.sql with tables, constraints, indexes, triggers, and RLS policies. Validation blocker: local Supabase/Docker unavailable (supabase db lint failed: cannot connect to Docker daemon).

title: Implement review media storage pipeline with multi-image upload
description:
Add secure upload/delete flow for review media.

Requirements
- Multiple image attachments per review (configurable max count)
- File type/size/dimension validation
- Deterministic sort ordering
- Safe public URL generation
- Owner/admin remove-image moderation path
- Cleanup of orphaned uploads on failed submissions
acceptance_criteria:
- Upload endpoint enforces validation limits
- Review can persist ordered list of media assets
- Failed/abandoned uploads are cleaned up deterministically

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented review media pipeline: added POST /api/reviews/media/upload-url, /complete, and /cleanup routes with trusted-origin enforcement, active-store resolution, signed upload URL generation, MIME/size/dimension validation, max-image enforcement, deterministic ordering, and draft-orphan cleanup. Expanded lib/reviews/media helpers for configurable limits, draft path/prefix ownership checks, storage listing, and stable reindexing. Added tests in apps/web/tests/review-media.test.ts plus env knobs in .env.example and apps/web/lib/env.ts. Validation: npm run test --workspace=@myrivo/web -- review-media review-abuse; npm run lint --workspace=@myrivo/web; npm run typecheck --workspace=@myrivo/web.

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Status correction: implementation complete and validated; forcing close despite upstream myrivo-29.2 still blocked on local Docker-based migration runtime validation.

title: Build customer review submission APIs with verified purchase checks
description:
Create APIs for submitting store/product reviews and attaching media refs.

Rules
- Verified purchase inference from order linkage and customer email
- Duplicate prevention policy (one per order+product+reviewer policy as specified)
- Rate limiting and idempotency guards
- Content validation (rating bounds, text lengths, prohibited payloads)
- Initial moderation status assignment
acceptance_criteria:
- Verified purchase flag is correct for eligible submissions
- Duplicate and spam attempts rejected with deterministic errors
- API returns normalized created review resource

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented POST /api/reviews with verified-purchase inference (orders + order_items by store/email/product), duplicate rejection, rate-limit/idempotency guards, validation/sanitization, moderation reason assignment, and media persistence against staged uploads. Returns normalized review resource with media list.

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Added apps/web/app/api/reviews/route.ts implementing customer submission flow with verified purchase inference from orders/order_items, deterministic duplicate prevention (fingerprint + DB unique constraint handling), per-IP/per-email rate limits, moderation heuristics hold/reason assignment, content sanitization, media attachment persistence, and orphan media cleanup on failure. Validation: lint/typecheck pass and review-abuse/review-media tests pass.

title: Build reviews read APIs for storefront with filters, sorting, and pagination
description:
Implement read endpoints for store/product review lists and summary aggregates.

Capabilities
- Filter by rating, verified-only, has-media
- Sort by newest, highest, lowest, most-helpful (if enabled)
- Cursor or offset pagination with stable ordering
- Summary payload (avg rating, counts, distribution)
- Status enforcement: only published reviews publicly visible
acceptance_criteria:
- Public APIs return only published reviews
- Pagination is stable under concurrent inserts
- Aggregate values match canonical review rows

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented public read APIs: GET /api/reviews/store/[storeSlug] and GET /api/reviews/product/[productId] with rating/verifiedOnly/hasMedia filters, newest/highest/lowest sorting, cursor pagination, and summary payload (review_count, average_rating, rating buckets). Added shared query utilities in apps/web/lib/reviews/read.ts.

title: Implement moderation APIs and owner response workflow
description:
Create owner/admin moderation endpoints and owner reply actions.

Features
- Queue views by status (pending/reported/rejected/published)
- Approve/reject/restore transitions with audit metadata
- Remove or hide individual review images
- Owner response create/update/delete with moderation constraints
- Bulk moderation actions for queue triage
acceptance_criteria:
- Unauthorized users cannot moderate
- All moderation actions recorded in audit trail
- Owner response visible only when parent review is published

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented moderation APIs and owner response workflow: GET /api/dashboard/reviews (queue with filters), PATCH /api/dashboard/reviews/[reviewId]/moderation (publish/reject/restore with transition checks and moderation audit metadata), PATCH /api/dashboard/reviews/[reviewId]/media/[mediaId] (hide/remove/restore), and PUT/DELETE /api/dashboard/reviews/[reviewId]/response (published-only response upsert/delete). Store-scoped auth via owned-store bundle and trusted-origin enforcement added on mutating routes. Validation: @myrivo/web lint/typecheck/tests pass.

title: Storefront UI: product/store review modules with media gallery
description:
Implement storefront review UX for product and store contexts.

UX
- Rating summary card and distribution bars
- Review list with filters/sort/pagination
- Verified purchase badge and owner response rendering
- Multi-image thumbnail strip + lightbox
- Empty/loading/error states and mobile-first behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- Product and store pages both render reviews correctly
- Media gallery supports multiple images per review
- Accessibility checks pass for interactive controls

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented storefront review modules with media gallery and submission flow. Added reusable client component apps/web/components/storefront/storefront-reviews-section.tsx and integrated it into store home and product detail pages. Component loads summary + paged published reviews from new APIs, renders media gallery, and supports customer submission with multi-image upload (upload-url -> object upload -> complete -> review submit). Validation: @myrivo/web lint/typecheck/tests pass.

title: Dashboard UI: moderation inbox, detail drawer, and response tools
description:
Build owner-facing moderation workspace for reviews and media.

UX
- Queue tabs by moderation status
- Review detail panel with media moderation controls
- Inline owner response editor
- Bulk action controls and optimistic UI handling
- Filter by product, rating, verified status, has-media
acceptance_criteria:
- Owners can process moderation queue end-to-end
- Media moderation actions update state correctly
- UI reflects audit-backed status transitions

title: Notifications + email integration for review lifecycle events
description:
Integrate reviews into notification system.

Events
- New review submitted (owner alert)
- Low-rating review threshold alert
- Review approved/rejected (optional customer notice if policy enables)
- Owner response posted (customer notice if account-linked)

Include channel preference mapping and dedupe strategy.
acceptance_criteria:
- Owner receives in-app notifications for new reviews
- Email channel obeys preferences and sender/reply rules
- Dedupe prevents duplicate lifecycle alerts

comments.author: Mike Baldwin
comments.text: Implemented review lifecycle notifications via existing dispatcher. Added catalog events: review.created.owner, review.low_rating.owner, review.responded.customer, review.moderated.customer. Added owner/customer dispatch helpers in owner-notifications.ts and wired them into review submission, moderation, and response APIs. Updated owner-notifications tests and validated with notification + review test suites.

title: Platform expansion program (pickup intelligence, roles, customer accounts, billing, white-label, marketing, test mode)
description: Execute full platform expansion across checkout pickup intelligence, role model, customer accounts, subscription/fees, white-labeling, test mode, and public marketing site.
acceptance_criteria: All scoped capabilities are implemented with migrations, APIs, UI, tests, runbooks, and rollout guardrails.
close_reason: All child beads myrivo-3.1 through myrivo-3.15 are completed and merged to develop.

title: Program architecture freeze and ADRs
description: Finalize specs and ADRs for pickup intelligence, role model, billing, customer accounts, test mode, and white-labeling.
acceptance_criteria: Approved architecture docs and acceptance matrix committed.

title: Billing admin and reporting UI
description: Add admin controls and merchant billing visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Billing controls/reporting available with role gating.
notes: Completed billing dashboard reporting with reconciliation issue detection, profile snapshot, billing events visibility, and permission-gated admin controls.
close_reason: Merged PR #47 to develop with billing reporting UI and reconciliation checks.

title: Public Myrivo marketing website
description: Build public marketing pages and conversion funnel to auth.
acceptance_criteria: Public site is live with CTA flow instrumentation.
close_reason: Added public Myrivo marketing page with signup/signin CTA flow.

title: Sandbox test mode architecture
description: Implement store sandbox/live mode and environment-safe key behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox mode is isolated with visible indicators and safe key routing.
close_reason: Implemented store and billing test mode controls in schema and settings UI/API.

title: White-label domain and favicon system
description: Implement custom domain and per-tenant favicon/brand metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Tenant storefront resolves custom domain and brand assets correctly.

title: Auth UX completion
description: Ensure navigation and UX between create-account and sign-in paths.
acceptance_criteria: Auth flows are linked and unambiguous.
close_reason: Completed auth UX navigation between create account and sign in.

title: Migration hardening and rollout
description: Execute migration checks, observability, and rollout runbooks.
acceptance_criteria: Rollout checklist complete with integrity checks and ops docs.
notes: Core migration hardening completed; remaining rollout step is DB apply + backfill validation against live project.
close_reason: Added automated platform rollout integrity verifier, updated runbooks/checklists, applied owner-membership backfill migration, and validated rollout against live DB.

title: Role model and RLS hardening
description: Implement platform/store role model and enforce role-based API+RLS controls.
acceptance_criteria: Platform/store role matrix enforced in code and DB policies.
close_reason: Implemented permission-aware store authorization (requireStorePermission), permissions_json override support, and route guard hardening for members/domains/billing APIs with tests.

title: Pickup schema and scheduling primitives
description: Implement pickup settings/locations/schedules/blackouts schema and slot generation library.
acceptance_criteria: Store pickup config and scheduling primitives available with tests.
close_reason: Implemented pickup schema foundation, distance/slot engine modules, and tests.

title: Pickup checkout integration
description: Integrate pickup eligibility and slot selection into checkout.
acceptance_criteria: Checkout enforces radius and lead-time behavior for pickup.

title: Pickup admin UI and email updates
description: Add dashboard UI for pickup configuration and include pickup details in confirmation emails.
acceptance_criteria: Merchants configure pickup end-to-end and buyers receive location/time details in emails.

title: Customer account model and APIs
description: Implement customer profile, saved stores/items, and persistent carts APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Customer account state persists and APIs are tenant-safe.

title: Customer dashboard UI
description: Build customer dashboard for orders, saved stores/items, and cart.
acceptance_criteria: Customers can manage saved and order history data from account UI.

title: Merchant profile and account surface
description: Add profile/account page in bottom sidebar and account settings UI.
acceptance_criteria: Merchant profile page is discoverable and functional.
close_reason: Implemented editable dashboard Profile & Account settings (identity + preferences) with authenticated API and nav active-state.

title: Billing plans and transaction fee engine
description: Implement plans, fee calculation, and order fee snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: Fees are computed deterministically and exposed in reporting.

title: Admin Workspace hardening and expansion
description: Create a dedicated admin workspace with accurate platform metrics, store governance controls, moderation queue, and platform audit visibility for launch readiness.

title: Admin IA and routing: dedicated /dashboard/admin workspace
description: Add dedicated admin workspace route structure, migrate platform console entrypoint to admin home, update navigation, and keep backward-compatible redirect from /dashboard/platform.

title: Platform overview metrics correctness and scalability
description: Refactor platform overview API to use accurate aggregate counts (not limited list reductions) and provide queue-oriented summary data for admin overview cards.

title: Store governance controls: reasoned reject/suspend and decision timeline
description: Require structured reasons for reject/suspend actions, persist governance metadata, and expose decision history in admin store governance view.

title: Platform moderation queue across stores
description: Add admin moderation page and API for cross-store pending review/media moderation signals, with direct links into store review workspace for actioning.

title: Platform audit explorer page
description: Add platform-wide audit API and admin page to filter and inspect governance-relevant actions by actor, entity, and store.

title: Admin workspace verification and regression tests
description: Add/update tests for admin routes and APIs, then run lint/typecheck/tests for touched surfaces.

title: Growth+Compliance Program: Analytics, Marketing Site, Legal Acceptance, and Docs
description: Program umbrella for four launch-critical tracks: storefront analytics for merchants, conversion-focused public marketing website, versioned legal acceptance capture, and a full documentation system. This program coordinates dependencies, rollout sequencing, QA, and release readiness across tracks.

title: Storefront Analytics Module: traffic, funnel, attribution, and owner dashboard
description: Build a first-party analytics module that tracks storefront traffic and customer behavior, attributes conversions, rolls up metrics, and presents actionable owner-facing analytics in store dashboard workspaces.

title: Public Website Conversion Rebuild: high-converting acquisition experience
description: Rebuild the Myrivo public website to function as a conversion engine with clear positioning, proof, plan clarity, and measurable CTA funnels.

title: Dashboard fixed-shell UX migration
description: Migrate dashboard to fixed header + fixed sidebar + independent content scrolling model with desktop-app feel.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shell is fixed and consistent across routes with no body-scroll bleed, validated by tests and QA checklist.
close_reason: All child beads 4.1 through 4.11 completed and merged to develop.

title: Baseline audit and migration guardrails
description: Capture current layout behavior, sticky/fixed inventory, layering risks, and acceptance matrix before shell refactor.
acceptance_criteria: Migration plan and baseline audit docs committed with explicit guardrails and verification checklist.
close_reason: Added dashboard shell migration plan and baseline audit/QA matrix docs.

title: Automated regression coverage for shell
description: Add tests for shell structure, independent scroll ownership, and sticky behavior regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests cover fixed-shell behaviors and pass in CI.
notes: Added dashboard fixed-shell Playwright regression spec covering desktop shell structure and mobile drawer behavior; validated with lint/typecheck and targeted e2e run on isolated port.
close_reason: Automated shell regression coverage added and validated.

title: Rollout and verification closeout
description: Run rollout checklist, cross-device QA, and docs finalization for fixed-shell migration.
acceptance_criteria: Migration is validated and documented with sign-off checklist.
notes: Published rollout closeout doc with completed bead matrix, verification summary, and residual environment dependency notes for e2e execution.
close_reason: Fixed-shell rollout verification completed and documented.

title: Dashboard shell container refactor
description: Introduce full-height dashboard shell with explicit header/body regions and overflow ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard layout uses viewport-height shell with body scroll disabled at shell level.
notes: Converted dashboard layout to viewport-owned shell (`h-[100dvh]`) with explicit flex/grid regions and main content overflow ownership.
close_reason: Refactored dashboard shell container and overflow ownership in app/dashboard/layout.tsx with lint/typecheck passing.

title: Fixed top header migration
description: Move top dashboard context/header into fixed shell header with stable height and actions.
acceptance_criteria: Header remains visible during content scroll and retains existing store context/actions.
notes: Stabilized shell header row as fixed dashboard context region and moved storefront action into header next to status indicator.
close_reason: Migrated dashboard top header actions/context and removed duplicate storefront action from sidebar.

title: Sidebar fixed-column and segmented scrolling
description: Pin sidebar column with top/store switcher and bottom/account sections fixed while center nav scrolls.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar supports long nav content without affecting page scroll and preserves section UX behaviors.
notes: Refactored sidebar into fixed top/bottom sections with independent middle navigation scroll region using flex + min-height constraints.
close_reason: Implemented segmented sidebar scrolling in dashboard-nav with top and bottom pinned sections.

title: Main content pane scroll migration
description: Ensure main content area is the primary scroll surface and page-level sticky headers are container-aware.
acceptance_criteria: Main content scroll works independently with sticky page headers behaving correctly.
notes: Annotated dashboard main content pane as explicit scroll container and migrated sticky page-header shadow detection to container-aware listeners.
close_reason: Implemented main pane scroll container contract and container-aware DashboardPageHeader sticky-state logic.

title: Layering and z-index contract enforcement
description: Standardize z-index layering for shell, sticky elements, tables, menus, and modals to prevent overlap regressions.
acceptance_criteria: No sticky/header overlap regressions across catalog/orders/billing/platform.
notes: Enforced dashboard z-index contract: page sticky headers now outrank sticky table columns; documented canonical layer map in dashboard-shell-layer-contract.md.
close_reason: Standardized shell/table/popover/dialog layering and fixed catalog sticky overlap regression.

title: Responsive fallback shell behavior
description: Implement tablet/mobile behavior for fixed shell (collapsible or drawer nav) while preserving usability.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard remains fully usable on mobile/tablet without dual-scroll traps.
notes: Implemented mobile/tablet dashboard navigation drawer fallback with desktop sidebar retained at lg+ breakpoints to prevent long inline nav stacks and dual-scroll traps.
close_reason: Added sheet-based mobile nav flow and desktop-only pinned sidebar behavior.

title: Dashboard route compatibility sweep
description: Route-by-route fixes for overflow, sticky tables, and interaction issues under new shell.
acceptance_criteria: All dashboard routes render and scroll correctly under fixed-shell model.
notes: Completed dashboard route compatibility sweep and fixed Select primitive slot rendering that triggered React.Fragment style-prop runtime warnings on dashboard pages.
close_reason: Route sweep completed with shell-compatibility fix for dashboard Select usage.

title: Accessibility and keyboard behavior hardening
description: Verify focus, keyboard nav, and aria behavior in fixed shell and collapsible nav contexts.
acceptance_criteria: Keyboard-only and assistive tech flows are preserved across navigation and overlays.
notes: Added accessibility hardening for fixed shell navigation: controlled mobile sheet close behavior and aria-current semantics across dashboard navigation links.
close_reason: Completed keyboard/screen-reader hardening for dashboard shell navigation.

title: Legal Versioning + Acceptance Ledger: Terms/Privacy consent enforcement
description: Implement versioned legal document management and immutable acceptance tracking across signup/login/store workflows, with re-consent gates and auditability.

title: Documentation Platform + Content Expansion: merchant, admin, and operator docs
description: Replace lightweight static docs with a robust docs system and complete task-oriented documentation coverage for launch and support readiness.

title: Analytics schema + ingestion API foundation
description:
Create analytics data model and ingestion pipeline: storefront_sessions, storefront_events, and storefront_daily_rollups tables, plus POST /api/analytics/collect endpoint with validation, idempotency, and abuse guards.

Acceptance criteria:
- Migration adds session/event/rollup tables with indexes and RLS policy strategy
- Ingestion endpoint validates event schema and rejects malformed payloads
- Session correlation works across page navigation in storefront
- Basic unit tests cover schema validation and idempotency

title: Storefront first-party event SDK + instrumentation rollout
description:
Implement a first-party client analytics SDK/helper and instrument storefront behavior events: page_view, product_view, add_to_cart, cart_view, checkout_started, checkout_completed, newsletter_subscribed, search_performed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Storefront route surfaces emit standardized events
- Event payloads include store slug/id and canonical path
- Checkout completion is linked to order id when available
- Instrumentation toggle exists for safe rollout

title: Attribution model and conversion linkage
description:
Capture UTM/referrer attribution fields (first-touch and last-touch) and propagate session identity to checkout/order records for conversion attribution.

Acceptance criteria:
- UTM/referrer fields persisted and queryable
- Order/session linkage stored for paid orders
- Attribution survives across cart/checkout transition
- Tests validate attribution persistence

title: Analytics query services + rollup jobs
description:
Build query services and rollup jobs for analytics KPIs, funnels, product/page performance, and channel breakdowns with date filters and comparison windows.

Acceptance criteria:
- Daily rollups populate correctly
- Service functions expose overview/funnel/channel/product/page reports
- Query performance acceptable on representative data volume
- Tests cover rollup correctness and query outputs

title: Owner analytics dashboard UI (/dashboard/stores/[storeSlug]/analytics)
description:
Create a dedicated analytics dashboard page for store owners with KPI cards, traffic/revenue trends, funnel, top products/pages, source attribution, and export actions.

Acceptance criteria:
- New route integrated with store workspace nav
- Date range + compare filters supported
- Empty/loading/error states implemented
- CSV export works for core tables

title: Analytics privacy, retention, and plan-tier controls
description:
Add data governance and monetization controls: retention windows, plan-gated features, privacy-safe payload handling, and anti-bot/rate limit protections.

Acceptance criteria:
- No PII persisted in analytics events by default
- Retention jobs documented and test-covered
- Plan gating enforced in API/UI surfaces
- Operational runbook added

title: Marketing IA + messaging architecture
description:
Define conversion-focused information architecture and messaging hierarchy for the public website, including ICP-specific value propositions and CTA strategy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Page map for home/features/pricing/compare/customers/contact
- Messaging matrix for core personas and objections
- CTA hierarchy and conversion goals defined

title: Homepage conversion redesign
description:
Redesign / with high-conversion structure: strong hero, value proof, product walkthrough, social proof, pricing teaser, objection-handling FAQ, and dominant CTA flow.

Acceptance criteria:
- Responsive and accessible implementation
- Clear above-the-fold CTA and supporting proof
- Structured SEO metadata and schema markup baseline
- Event tracking hooks for key conversion actions

title: Deep hardening wave: architecture, reliability, UX, and tests
description: Run a comprehensive hardening wave across dashboard/app code: remove architectural hotspots, improve reliability/error handling, close UX consistency gaps, and add missing tests for recently shipped features.

title: Features and compare pages
description:
Implement /features and /compare with clear capability narratives, differentiation, and conversion CTAs mapped to user intent stages.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pages launched with consistent design system
- Copy maps to platform/store-owner outcomes
- CTAs tracked and routed correctly

title: Pricing page overhaul + trust/risk reducers
description:
Transform /pricing into a decision page with plan comparison matrix, transparent fee explanation, FAQs, and trust messaging to reduce purchase anxiety.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plan matrix accuracy validated against billing config
- FAQ addresses common objections
- CTA paths to signup/contact measured

title: SEO landing pages + content growth baseline
description:
Add high-intent SEO landing pages and content templates for acquisition queries; include technical SEO hardening and internal linking strategy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initial keyword-targeted pages live
- Sitemap/internal links updated
- Search console measurement plan documented

title: Marketing analytics + CRO experimentation loop
description:
Implement marketing event instrumentation and a lightweight experiment framework for iterative conversion optimization.

Acceptance criteria:
- Funnel events tracked from visit to signup
- Dashboard/reporting for conversion by page and CTA
- Experiment process documented

title: Legal documents and version tables
description:
Create legal document/version data model: legal_documents, legal_document_versions, and legal_acceptances with immutable acceptance evidence fields (timestamp, user, version hash, surface).

Acceptance criteria:
- Migration + RLS policies implemented
- Version publishing model documented
- Acceptance records immutable by policy

title: Signup acceptance capture (terms + privacy)
description:
Add mandatory legal acceptance controls to signup with explicit consent language and stored version identifiers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Signup blocked unless required boxes checked
- Acceptance rows created with current version ids
- UI links to active legal docs

title: Re-consent gate for updated required legal versions
description:
Implement middleware/app gate requiring users to accept newly required legal versions before continuing to app routes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Users with stale consent are redirected to consent screen
- Acceptance completion returns user to intended path
- Tests cover gating and bypass rules

title: Admin legal publishing and acceptance audit UI
description:
Build admin/legal management UI to create/publish legal versions and inspect acceptance records for compliance/audit response.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin can publish new versions with effective dates
- Acceptance lookup by user/store/version works
- Export/report capability available

title: Legal communication + operational runbook
description:
Define and implement communication mechanics for material legal updates (in-app + email), plus incident/runbook guidance for legal disputes and evidence requests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notification templates for legal update messages
- Runbook added under docs/runbooks
- Owner/admin guidance documented

title: Docs platform migration (from in-code static to content-driven)
description:
Migrate docs content from hardcoded TypeScript structures to content-driven markdown/MDX with typed frontmatter and rendering pipeline.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing docs pages render from content files
- Category/nav metadata preserved or improved
- Tests validate content schema and route generation

title: Harden avatar lifecycle (staging, cleanup, discard/save semantics)
description: Ensure avatar uploads cannot be silently lost or orphaned. Preserve clear save/discard semantics, clean up temporary uploads, and add robust API failure handling paths.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Merchant docs expansion
description:
Author complete merchant-facing docs for onboarding, catalog, orders, pickup/shipping, domains, emails, reviews, analytics, and troubleshooting.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each critical merchant workflow has a doc
- Cross-links and prerequisites included
- Reviewed for factual consistency with current UI

title: Admin/operator docs expansion
description:
Create admin/operator docs for moderation, approvals, governance, incident response, deployment/release operations, and support playbooks.

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin workspace pages all have supporting docs
- Incident and escalation paths are explicit
- Runbooks reference real commands/routes

title: In-app contextual help integration
description:
Add context-aware help entry points from key dashboard/store settings/workflow screens into relevant docs pages.

Acceptance criteria:
- Help links present on high-friction screens
- Links deep-link to specific docs anchors/sections
- Basic usage telemetry captured

title: Docs governance: ownership, freshness SLAs, and release checklist integration
description:
Establish docs ownership and freshness process, and integrate docs updates into PR/release readiness standards.

Acceptance criteria:
- Doc owner + review cadence metadata exists
- Release checklist includes docs verification gates
- Stale-doc detection process documented

title: Stabilize generated typings and repository hygiene
description: Eliminate unstable generated-file churn (e.g., next-env dev route import), ensure deterministic typing file state, and add guardrails so local dev does not dirty develop unexpectedly.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Refactor dashboard product manager into modular units
description: Break the oversized product manager component into focused hooks/components, remove hook-dependency suppressions, and preserve all current behaviors with tests.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.

title: Standardize API request parsing and safe error responses
description: Harden API routes by adding safe JSON parsing and consistent error responses (especially around profile/store settings mutations) while preserving current behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to develop via PRs #64-#68.
